science.title: Scientists just found a hidden weakness in forever chemicals
science.summary: Researchers discovered that hydrogen radicals generated by intense UV light can break down stubborn PFAS “forever chemicals” without added chemicals. The breakthrough reveals a key mechanism that could lead to greener and more effective technologies for permanently destroying these pollutants.

science.title: Scientists discover an earthquake gate as California faults reach their highest stress levels in 1,000 years
science.summary: A new study suggests Southern California's major fault system is more stressed than at any point in the last 1,000 years. Researchers found that the Cajon Pass, where the San Andreas and San Jacinto faults meet, could act as an “earthquake gate” that determines whether a future rupture spreads across both faults. Curre

science.title: The first primates may have evolved in the cold, not the tropics
science.summary: A surprising new study suggests the earliest primates didn't originate in tropical forests but in cold, dry parts of North America. Some may have even survived seasonal Arctic conditions by slowing their metabolism or hibernating. Researchers found that dramatic climate shifts, rather than warmth, played a major role i

science.title: Scientists Capture Immune Cells Eating Live Cancer Cells for the First Time
science.summary: Scientists discovered and filmed macrophages actively destroying live melanoma cells, a finding that could improve immunotherapy and inspire new cancer treatments. Scientists at the Garvan Institute of Medical Research have, for the first time, recorded immune cells known as the body’s “housekeepers”

science.title: Near Absolute Zero, This Transistor Starts Acting Like a Brain Cell
science.summary: A single transistor that behaves like a brain cell in the deep freeze could help unlock the next generation of quantum computers and space exploration systems. Researchers at the University of Hong Kong (HKU) have developed a new type of brain-inspired electronic hardware that can operate at temperatures close to absol

tech.title: He made your free video player run smoothly. Now he’s doing that for robots.
tech.summary: French serial entrepreneur and open-source legend Jean-Baptiste Kempf has been building Kyber, an infrastructure layer to control remote devices in real time.

tech.title: The Download: a reality check for geoengineering and the science of interoception
tech.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. tech.title: The UK will scan asylum-seekers’ faces for age checks—despite knowing the tech is flawed
tech.summary: Tests of age-verification technology show the risks of life-altering errors.

tech.title: TechCrunch Mobility: A new robotaxi scorecard shows China’s dominance
tech.summary: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility — your central hub for news and insights on the future of transportation.

tech.title: Founders Fund’s outlier bet on humanely killed fish
tech.summary: Shinkei makes a refrigerator-sized robot called Poseidon to kill fish quickly and humanely.

medicine.title: Most people who stop GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic eventually return
medicine.summary: Many people prescribed GLP-1 drugs for type 2 diabetes (such as Victoza, Ozempic, or tirzepatide) stop taking them, but a surprising number later return to treatment. Researchers found that newer medications appear to keep patients on therapy longer, while side effects remain a major reason for discontinuation.

medicine.title: As patients move through injury care, well-intentioned changes can backfire
medicine.summary: Global health policymakers should focus on the way entire health care systems work together, argue researchers in new findings. Improving one element does not always create better outcomes if the wider system is not ready.

medicine.title: Only a third of eligible breast cancer patients receive a gene expression test
medicine.summary: A gene expression test can help prevent unnecessary chemotherapy in breast cancer patients, yet its use in the Netherlands remains limited. This is shown by new research from the Netherlands Comprehensive Cancer Centre (IKNL), led by Sabine Siesling, principal investigator at IKNL and professor at the University of Twe

medicine.title: New study attempts to reconcile conflicting findings on benefits vs. risks of alcohol consumption
medicine.summary: Studies of alcohol's effects on health have offered contradictory findings, with some suggesting a glass of red wine a day is beneficial and others saying even a drop of booze is too much. A new review attempting to clarify the risks finds more than 60 diseases, based on the World Health Organization's International Cl

medicine.title: Long-lived families reveal a rare genetic clue to healthy aging
medicine.summary: A study of long-lived families has identified rare genetic variants that may help people stay healthier for much longer as they age. One standout mutation appears to temper inflammation, potentially delaying disease and extending years of healthy living.

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win 2026 Breakthrough, New Horizons prizes
mathematics.summary: Faculty member Shu-Heng Shao, in addition to four MIT alumni, are honored by the Breakthrough Prize Foundation.

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: Seven Perfect Shuffles Randomize a Deck of Cards. But How Many Sloppy Ones?
mathematics.summary: A decades-old proof showed that seven shuffles are enough to mix up a deck of cards. But it requires you to cut the deck with the precision of a professional magician. A new proof gets around that obstacle. The post Seven Perfect Shuffles Randomize a Deck of Cards. But How Many Sloppy Ones? first appeared on Quanta Mag

mathematics.title: How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math
mathematics.summary: With automated proof-checkers, a problem can be broken up into small chunks, solved bit-by-bit, then reassembled with confidence that every piece is correct. For some, this heralds a new area in mathematical research. The post How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up
mathematics.summary: By replacing the most fundamental concept in topology, Peter Scholze and Dustin Clausen are taking the first step in a far bigger program to understand why numbers behave the way they do. The post Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up first appeared on Quanta Magazine

space.title: A private company will build and launch NASA's next Mars orbiter in 2028 — and it's not SpaceX
space.summary: A California-based company will design, build and launch a spacecraft for NASA's Aeolus mission to orbit around Mars, where the probe will be the first to provide daily measurements of the planet's global environment.

space.title: NASA Mission to Study Space Weather Impacts of Earth’s Atmosphere
space.summary: NASA selected a mission concept to research how space weather and dynamics within Earth’s atmosphere influence the space environment and help improve prediction capabilities for impacts on crucial technology, such as GPS and low Earth orbit satellites, as well as astronauts in space. The DAPHNE (Dynamic Atmosphere-Iono

space.title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launches 24 Starlink satellites into low Earth orbit from California
space.summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket carrying 24 Starlink satellites lifted off from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California on Sunday, June 21, 2026.

space.title: These Mars valleys are hiding 30 dust devils | Space photo of the day for June 18, 2026
space.summary: Europe's Mars Express orbiter recently imaged a huge valley system on the Red Planet — and dust devils dot the alien landscape.

space.title: 'Best. Mars. Mission. Ever.' Scientists hail MAVEN's legacy as NASA retires Red Planet orbiter
space.summary: NASA's MAVEN Mars mission ended after 11 years, having revealed how the planet lost its atmosphere and served as a key communications relay for surface missions.

astronomy.title: “Shadow Blaster” Galaxy Might Have Sent High-Energy Neutrino to Earth
astronomy.summary: A star-forming galaxy in the early universe might have sent a ghostly particle known as a neutrino crashing into the ice at Earth’s South Pole, after an 11 billion-year journey through space. The post “Shadow Blaster” Galaxy Might Have Sent High-Energy Neutrino to Earth appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers extreme exoplanet being roasted by its home star
astronomy.summary: Who ordered the roasted exoplanet? Astronomers using the James Webb Space Telescope found a world that really puts the "hot" in "Hot Jupiter."

astronomy.title: Did A Moon-Size Planet Grow Fast and Die Young in the Early Solar System?
astronomy.summary: A rare meteorite recovered from the Sahara Desert could be a fragment of a Moon-size body that met a violent end in the earliest days of the solar system. The post Did A Moon-Size Planet Grow Fast and Die Young in the Early Solar System? appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: This Week's Sky at a Glance, June 12 – 21
astronomy.summary: The three planets in the western twilight are pulling away from each other now. On Wednesday, the Moon will occult Venus in daylight. The post This Week's Sky at a Glance, June 12 – 21 appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Astronomers Want to Build a Swarm of Telescopes to Find LIFE
astronomy.summary: Current plans for flagship telescopes in the 2040s are focused on answering a simple question - are we alone? Our best telescopes to date, such as the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) have only given us tantalizing glimpses into the atmospheres or other worlds, but not enough to truly determine whether or not life as

architecture.title: Designing for Stray Cities: Architecture Beyond the Human
architecture.summary: Architecture continues to draw cities as though humans occupy them alone. Plans trace circulation routes, zoning maps assign functions, and buildings are evaluated according to human comfort, safety, and efficiency. Walking through cities across India and Southwest Asia reveals something much more complex. Dogs sleep b

architecture.title: BIG designs "new kind of urban university" for Bentonville
architecture.summary: Architecture studio BIG has designed a series of buildings for a university being established by the members of the Walton family in Bentonville, Arkansas, USA with three structures that expand on the local vernacular. Members of the Walton family, who are best known as founders of retail giant Walmart, announced that

architecture.title: City-Making Through Participation: Lessons from Utopian Hours 2026
architecture.summary: Who has the right to the city? Henri Lefebvre's writings question the structures that control urban space and, instead, put the citizens at the center of decision-making. His ideas have influenced the way architecture and urban design are practiced, bringing about community participation and co-design. These have been

architecture.title: Schmidt Hammer Lassen Completes ARoS Expansion with James Turrell's As Seen Below – The Dome
architecture.summary: Denmark's ARoS Aarhus Art Museum has unveiled As Seen Below – The Dome, a new Skyspace by American artist James Turrell that completes The Next Level, the museum's approximately 4,000-square-metre underground expansion designed by Schmidt Hammer Lassen. Opened to the public on 19 June 2026, the project marks the culmin

architecture.title: Herzog & de Meuron designs own offices in Basel as a "repository of materials"
architecture.summary: Architecture studio Herzog & de Meuron has completed Mailand-Strasse, a headquarters for its own teams in Basel built using a palette of exposed timber, concrete and metal informed by "logistics and infrastructure". The 7,259-square-metre building gives Herzog & de Meuron a permanent base in Basel's Dreispitz q

construction.title: Virginia approves 6-year, $28.5B infrastructure plan
construction.summary: The massive plan will fund more than 4,300 construction and infrastructure jobs across the state.

construction.title: Amazon plans $10B Missouri data center campus
construction.summary: In addition to the construction of the facility, the tech giant will improve nearby roads and water infrastructure in the state.

construction.title: Letter to the Editor: There's Nothing New Under the Sun for Bridges
construction.summary: I get a bit amused by articles on “new” construction methods for bridges, such as the following one in Ohio.

construction.title: Walsh-Turner JV gets green light on $580M utility plant project
construction.summary: The two construction firms add the University of Kentucky in Lexington project to the collaboration’s growing list of higher-ed jobs.

ai.title: Beyond LLMs: Why Scalable Enterprise AI Adoption Depends on Agent Logic
A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs
ai.summary: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic came out of stealth mode last month with a huge claim. It announced that it had solved a mathematical bottleneck that had been holding back large language models for almost a decade. The details were thin, and many people were unconvinced. ai.title: Using AI to help physicians diagnose rare genetic diseases affecting children
ai.summary: Researchers used an OpenAI reasoning model to help diagnose rare diseases, identifying 18 new diagnoses in previously unsolved cases.

ai.title: Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment
ai.summary: OpenAI introduces Deployment Simulation, a method to predict AI model behavior before deployment using real conversation data to improve safety and evaluation accuracy.

ai.title: New OpenAI Academy courses for the next era of work
ai.summary: OpenAI introduces three Academy courses that help people build practical AI skills, create repeatable workflows, and apply agents in everyday work.
